methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected data, including data standardization, ANOVA, hierarchical clustering analysis, and correlation analysis.

steps.description: Data standardization procedure by centering each variable around zero (i.e., subtracting the mean of the variable) and dividing by its standard deviation was done on both sets before data mining.
steps.searchable_summary: standardize data by centering and scaling

steps.description: Analysis of variances was applied for hypotheses testing to evaluate the significance of differences in the means of observed quantities of the tested bee products at chosen p ≤ 0.05.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ANOVA to test significance of mean differences

steps.description: Hierarchical clustering analysis allowed to present similarity among underlying groups of data using multilevel hierarchical structure, dendrogram, was applied on both prepared data sets-the data set representing physicochemical properties of the samples and the data set built from mineral analysis results.
To evaluate relationship between measured quantities describing samples Pearson's linear correlation coefficient was assessed at chosen p ≤ 0.05.

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing microelements in bee products using microwave-assisted extraction and inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry.

steps.description: Microwave-assisted extraction (MAE) was carried out using a CEM MARS 6 ® digestion system equipped with 100 mL Teflon vessel. Homogenised sample (0.3 g) was accurately weighed into the Teflon vessel and digested using 10 mL concentrated nitric acid and 2 mL concentrated hydrochloric acid.
Digestion was performed under following conditions: temperature 180°C, pressure 800 psi, ramp time 20 min, hold time 20 min, microwave power 800 W.
The digested sample was cooled down, thoroughly transferred into a 100 mL volumetric flask and diluted using bidistilled water to the mark.
Qualitative and quantitative evaluation of microelements was performed using inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometer (ICP-MS).

methods.method_name: Spectrophotometric Analysis of Bee Pollen and Other Bee Products
methods.description: Analysis of bioactive substances and radical scavenging activity in bee pollen and other bee products using spectrophotometry.

steps.description: The content of the most important bioactive substances (phenolic compounds and flavonoids) and radical scavenging activity in bee pollen and other bee product samples are presented.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze bioactive substances and radical scavenging activity

methods.description: Clustering analysis using hierarchical clustering to group samples based on physicochemical properties and mineral analysis results.

steps.description: Two dendrograms were built using HCA to group samples according to physicochemical properties and mineral analysis results.
